text.title: TikTok Basics for B2B/SaaS Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every TikTok Basics for B2B/SaaS application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Account MUST commit to 3-5 named content pillars before posting. Ad-hoc topical posts are rejected for the first 90 days.
rule.rationale: Algorithm anchors on pillar consistency; broad accounts under-perform niche accounts of equal effort.

rule.statement: Profile bio MUST carry one conversion line (link + value prop). Generic biographies are rejected.
rule.rationale: Bio is the only consistent CTA surface; without a conversion line the funnel leaks at the strongest moment.

rule.statement: Output MUST cover ≥3 of the named formats (tutorial / story / demo / day-in-life / hot-take) per month. Single-format accounts are rejected.
rule.rationale: Format variety lifts watch-time across viewer cohorts and stabilises reach.

rule.statement: Plan MUST define cadence by month (month 1: warm-up, month 2: scale, month 3+: optimisation). Indefinite ramp is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a ramp the operator under-posts in month 1 and over-promises in month 3.

rule.statement: Videos MUST ship in 9:16 vertical, ≤60s for the first 90 days. Landscape or long-form is rejected for the foundation phase.
rule.rationale: TikTok algorithm down-ranks non-native format; horizontal kills early reach.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
